text: I must not fear. Fear is the mind-killer. Fear is the little-death that brings total obliteration.
author: Frank Herbert

text: The mystery of life isn't a problem to solve, but a reality to experience.
author: Frank Herbert

text: Time is an illusion. Lunchtime doubly so.
author: Douglas Adams

text: Space is big. You just won't believe how vastly, hugely, mind-bogglingly big it is.
author: Douglas Adams

text: I love deadlines. I love the whooshing noise they make as they go by.
author: Douglas Adams

text: A common mistake when trying to design something completely foolproof is to underestimate the ingenuity of complete fools.
author: Douglas Adams

text: In the beginning the Universe was created. This has made a lot of people very angry and been widely regarded as a bad move.
author: Douglas Adams

text: Any sufficiently advanced technology is indistinguishable from magic.
author: Arthur C. Clarke

text: Two possibilities exist: either we are alone in the Universe or we are not. Both are equally terrifying.
author: Arthur C. Clarke

text: The future is already here — it's just not evenly distributed.
author: William Gibson

text: The sky above the port was the color of television, tuned to a dead channel.
author: William Gibson

text: Reality is that which, when you stop believing in it, doesn't go away.
author: Philip K. Dick

text: We are what we pretend to be, so we must be careful about what we pretend to be.
author: Kurt Vonnegut

text: Everything was beautiful and nothing hurt.
author: Kurt Vonnegut

text: You don't have to burn books to destroy a culture. Just get people to stop reading them.
author: Ray Bradbury

text: The true delight is in the finding out rather than in the knowing.
author: Isaac Asimov

text: The saddest aspect of life right now is that science gathers knowledge faster than society gathers wisdom.
author: Isaac Asimov

text: People who think they know everything are a great annoyance to those of us who do.
author: Isaac Asimov

text: Not all those who wander are lost.
author: J.R.R. Tolkien

text: Real stupidity beats artificial intelligence every time.
author: Terry Pratchett

text: Five exclamation marks, the sure sign of an insane mind.
author: Terry Pratchett

text: The truth may be out there, but the lies are inside your head.
author: Terry Pratchett

text: It's not worth doing something unless someone, somewhere, would much rather you weren't doing it.
author: Terry Pratchett

text: The most merciful thing in the world is the inability of the human mind to correlate all its contents.
author: H.P. Lovecraft

text: The only thing that makes life possible is permanent, intolerable uncertainty: not knowing what comes next.
author: Ursula K. Le Guin

text: The cosmos is within us. We are made of star-stuff.
author: Carl Sagan

text: Somewhere, something incredible is waiting to be known.
author: Carl Sagan

text: In science it often happens that scientists say, 'You know that's a really good argument; my position is mistaken.'
author: Carl Sagan

text: The universe is under no obligation to make sense to you.
author: Neil deGrasse Tyson

text: The good thing about science is that it's true whether or not you believe in it.
author: Neil deGrasse Tyson

text: The first principle is that you must not fool yourself — and you are the easiest person to fool.
author: Richard Feynman

text: I would rather have questions that can't be answered than answers that can't be questioned.
author: Richard Feynman

text: Physics is like sex: sure, it may give some practical results, but that's not why we do it.
author: Richard Feynman

text: Imagination is more important than knowledge.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Reality is merely an illusion, albeit a very persistent one.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Two things are infinite: the universe and human stupidity; and I'm not sure about the universe.
author: Albert Einstein

text: The most beautiful thing we can experience is the mysterious. It is the source of all true art and science.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Nothing in life is to be feared, it is only to be understood.
author: Marie Curie

text: We are just an advanced breed of monkeys on a minor planet of a very average star. But we can understand the Universe.
author: Stephen Hawking

text: Intelligence is the ability to adapt to change.
author: Stephen Hawking

text: Not only is the universe stranger than we think, it is stranger than we can think.
author: Werner Heisenberg

text: I think, therefore I am.
author: René Descartes

text: Hell is other people.
author: Jean-Paul Sartre

text: Man is condemned to be free.
author: Jean-Paul Sartre

text: One must imagine Sisyphus happy.
author: Albert Camus

text: I rebel; therefore I exist.
author: Albert Camus

text: The limits of my language are the limits of my world.
author: Ludwig Wittgenstein

text: Whereof one cannot speak, thereof one must be silent.
author: Ludwig Wittgenstein

text: Beware that, when fighting monsters, you yourself do not become a monster.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: He who has a why to live can bear almost any how.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: Without music, life would be a mistake.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: God is dead. God remains dead. And we have killed him.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: The only true wisdom is in knowing you know nothing.
author: Socrates

text: An unexamined life is not worth living.
author: Socrates

text: It is the mark of an educated mind to be able to entertain a thought without accepting it.
author: Aristotle

text: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
author: Aristotle

text: No man ever steps in the same river twice.
author: Heraclitus

text: Character is destiny.
author: Heraclitus

text: Out of the crooked timber of humanity, no straight thing was ever made.
author: Immanuel Kant

text: We suffer more often in imagination than in reality.
author: Seneca

text: Dwell on the beauty of life.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: The price of anything is the amount of life you exchange for it.
author: Henry David Thoreau

text: The mass of men lead lives of quiet desperation.
author: Henry David Thoreau

text: Courage is knowing what not to fear.
author: Plato

text: Those who cannot remember the past are condemned to repeat it.
author: George Santayana

text: The only way to make sense out of change is to plunge into it, move with it, and join the dance.
author: Alan Watts

text: Be yourself; everyone else is already taken.
author: Oscar Wilde

text: I am so clever that sometimes I don't understand a single word of what I am saying.
author: Oscar Wilde

text: I can resist everything except temptation.
author: Oscar Wilde

text: Always forgive your enemies; nothing annoys them so much.
author: Oscar Wilde

text: I am not young enough to know everything.
author: Oscar Wilde

text: The truth is rarely pure and never simple.
author: Oscar Wilde

text: The reports of my death are greatly exaggerated.
author: Mark Twain

text: I have never let my schooling interfere with my education.
author: Mark Twain

text: Be careful about reading health books. You may die of a misprint.
author: Mark Twain

text: Clothes make the man. Naked people have little or no influence on society.
author: Mark Twain

text: Outside of a dog, a book is man's best friend. Inside of a dog it's too dark to read.
author: Groucho Marx

text: I refuse to join any club that would have me as a member.
author: Groucho Marx

text: Those are my principles, and if you don't like them… well, I have others.
author: Groucho Marx

text: I find television very educating. Every time somebody turns on the set, I go into the other room and read a book.
author: Groucho Marx

text: If at first you don't succeed, then skydiving definitely isn't for you.
author: Steven Wright

text: I'm writing a book. I've got the page numbers done.
author: Steven Wright

text: The secret of being a bore is to tell everything.
author: Voltaire

text: Common sense is not so common.
author: Voltaire

text: Simplicity is the ultimate sophistication.
author: Leonardo da Vinci

text: In the middle of difficulty lies opportunity.
author: Albert Einstein

text: The only way to discover the limits of the possible is to go beyond them into the impossible.
author: Arthur C. Clarke

text: The ships hung in the sky in much the same way that bricks don't.
author: Douglas Adams

text: The first step in avoiding a trap is knowing it exists.
author: Frank Herbert

text: Seek freedom and become captive of your desires. Seek discipline and find your liberty.
author: Frank Herbert

text: A process cannot be understood by stopping it. Understanding must move with the flow of the process, must join it and flow with it.
author: Frank Herbert

text: It is good to have an end to journey toward; but it is the journey that matters, in the end.
author: Ursula K. Le Guin

text: Love doesn't just sit there like a stone; it has to be made, like bread, remade all the time, made new.
author: Ursula K. Le Guin

text: The creative adult is the child who survived.
author: Ursula K. Le Guin

text: The basic tool for the manipulation of reality is the manipulation of words.
author: Philip K. Dick

text: Don't try to solve serious matters in the middle of the night.
author: Philip K. Dick

text: We are an impossibility in an impossible universe.
author: Ray Bradbury

text: Go to the edge of the cliff and jump off. Build your wings on the way down.
author: Ray Bradbury

text: Violence is the last refuge of the incompetent.
author: Isaac Asimov

text: Give a man a fire and he's warm for a day, but set fire to him and he's warm for the rest of his life.
author: Terry Pratchett

text: In ancient times cats were worshipped as gods; they have not forgotten this.
author: Terry Pratchett

text: Man has gone out to explore other worlds and other civilizations without having explored his own labyrinth of dark passages and secret chambers.
author: Stanisław Lem

text: The universe is a dark forest. Every civilization is an armed hunter stalking through the trees like a ghost.
author: Liu Cixin

text: The only lasting truth is change.
author: Octavia Butler

text: In order to rise from its own ashes, a phoenix first must burn.
author: Octavia Butler

text: Fairy tales are more than true: not because they tell us that dragons exist, but because they tell us that dragons can be beaten.
author: Neil Gaiman

text: We are all just walking each other home.
author: Ram Dass

text: For small creatures such as we the vastness is bearable only through love.
author: Carl Sagan

text: We live in a society exquisitely dependent on science and technology, in which hardly anyone knows anything about science and technology.
author: Carl Sagan

text: Study hard what interests you the most in the most undisciplined, irreverent and original manner possible.
author: Richard Feynman

text: We are bits of stellar matter that got cold by accident, bits of a star gone wrong.
author: Arthur Eddington

text: The whole problem with the world is that fools and fanatics are always so certain of themselves, and wiser people so full of doubts.
author: Bertrand Russell

text: It ain't what you don't know that gets you into trouble. It's what you know for sure that just ain't so.
author: Mark Twain

text: I have always imagined that Paradise will be a kind of library.
author: Jorge Luis Borges

text: A dreamer is one who can only find his way by moonlight, and his punishment is that he sees the dawn before the rest of the world.
author: Oscar Wilde

text: An Outside Context Problem was the sort of thing most civilizations encountered just once, and didn't survive.
author: Iain M. Banks

text: The truth is usually just an excuse for a lack of imagination.
author: Iain M. Banks

text: Sleep is death without the responsibility.
author: Iain M. Banks

text: No dictator, no invader, can hold an imprisoned population by force of arms forever. There is no greater power in the universe than the need for freedom.
author: G'Kar — Babylon 5

text: The avalanche has already started. It is too late for the pebbles to vote.
author: Kosh Naranek — Babylon 5

text: My shoes are too tight. But it does not matter, because I have forgotten how to dance.
author: Londo Mollari — Babylon 5

text: Wouldn't it be worse if life were fair, and all the terrible things that happen to us came because we actually deserved them?
author: G'Kar — Babylon 5

text: Understanding is a three-edged sword: your side, their side, and the truth.
author: Kosh Naranek — Babylon 5

text: We are all the sum of our tears. Too little and the ground is not fertile, and nothing can grow there. Too much, the best of us is washed away.
author: G'Kar — Babylon 5

text: I have said it was unwise. I have not said that I will not do it.
author: Teal'c — Stargate SG-1

text: I'm going to have to science the shit out of this.
author: Mark Watney — The Martian

text: I don't want to come off as arrogant here, but I'm the greatest botanist on this planet.
author: Mark Watney — The Martian

text: Every human being has a basic instinct: to help each other out.
author: Andy Weir

text: Within thirty years, we will have the technological means to create superhuman intelligence. Shortly after, the human era will be ended.
author: Vernor Vinge

text: The best response to a world that doesn't care about you is to find the others who don't care about it either, and make something together.
author: Vernor Vinge

text: Your scientists were so preoccupied with whether or not they could, they didn't stop to think if they should.
author: Ian Malcolm — Jurassic Park

text: Life finds a way.
author: Ian Malcolm — Jurassic Park

text: I know exactly what I am.
author: Amos Burton — The Expanse

text: The Belt isn't a place you go. It's a place you end up.
author: Joe Miller — The Expanse

text: Every war in human history has just been one group of people trying to make another group of people die for their choices.
author: James Holden — The Expanse

text: I've been in politics my whole life. Long enough to know that power doesn't change who you are. It just shows you who you've always been.
author: Chrisjen Avasarala — The Expanse

text: You can take the girl out of the Belt. The Belt doesn't come out of the girl.
author: Naomi Nagata — The Expanse

text: The universe is not required to make sense to you.
author: James S.A. Corey

text: We are the Martians now.
author: Kim Stanley Robinson

text: The purpose of science fiction is not to predict the future, but to prevent it.
author: Kim Stanley Robinson

text: You are not thinking with your brain. You are thinking through it.
author: Peter Watts

text: Consciousness is a side effect of complexity, and complexity is what the universe builds when it has enough time.
author: Peter Watts

text: In a universe of blind physical forces and genetic replication, some people are going to get hurt, other people are going to get lucky, and you won't find any rhyme or reason in it.
author: Alastair Reynolds

text: The silence of the galaxy is not peaceful. It is the silence of a graveyard.
author: Alastair Reynolds
